Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant M.R. Carpenter, Inc.
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for application for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:35:31 a.m. and concluding at 10:02:21 a.m.

Plaintiff Eugene Kish represented by Kimberly Chu, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant M.R. Carpenter, Inc. not appearing.

Order Waiving Personal Appearance of All Witnesses re: Application for Default Judgment filed Apr-27-2023.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED FOR IDENTIFICATION:

Plaintiff's Exhibit E: Declaration of James P. Nevin
Plaintiff's Exhibit A: Medical Report of Richard Levy, M.D.
Plaintiff's Exhibit C: Complaint, filed September 21, 2018; Summons

Court finds the Complaint is the operative pleading filed on September 21, 2018 and DOES 1-800 were DISMISSED on January 27, 2023.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit A: Medical Report of Richard Levy, M.D.
Plaintiff's Exhibit D: Declaration of Plaintiff
Plaintiff's Exhibit F: Worksite Product indentification and summary of work place exposure
Plaintiff's Exhibit G: Declaration of Frank Ganzhorn, M.D.
Plaintiff's Exhibit E: Declaration of James P. Nevin

Based on the evidence provided, the declaration of James P. Nevin, the admission of Plaintiff's Exhibits A, D, F, G, and E, Court finds Plaintiff has provided evidence of the elements contained in the complaint. Defendant owed a duty to Plaintiff and Defendant breached that duty.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Eugene Kish, Inc., and against Defendant M.R. Carpenter, Inc., as follows:

1. Economic Damages: $90,750.00
2. Non- Economic Damages: $750,000.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $840,750.00

The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.6, this Court hereby retains jurisdiction to enforce this judgment, including but not limited to after the case is dismissed.

Proposed judgment signed.
